content.problemStatement:
A **valid split** is defined as splitting the array into contiguous, non-empty subsegments such that each element belongs to exactly one subsegment, and the GCD (Greatest Common Divisor) of the iii-th segment is equal to iii, where the subsegment numbering starts from 111 and goes up to kkk, with kkk being the total number of subsegments.

Let F(C)F(C)F(C) be a function on an array CCC = count of different ways to partition the array CCC such that it is a **valid split**.

Two partitions are considered different if the placement of the splits differs in any way, even if the values within the subsegments are the same.

Given an integer array AAA of length NNN, your task is to print the sum of F(B)F(B)F(B) over all subarrays BBB of the array AAA modulo 109+710^9 +7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, denoting the length of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,A3....AN A_1,A_2,A_3....A_NA1​,A2​,A3​....AN​ ,denoting the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of F(B)F(B)F(B) over all subarrays BBB of the array AAA modulo 109+710^9 +7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 111:

For [1][1][1] possible splits - [1][1][1]
For [2][2][2] possible splits - none
For [4][4][4] possible splits - none
For [1,2][1, 2][1,2] possible splits - [1,2][1, 2][1,2], [1][1][1] [2][2][2]
For [2,4][2, 4][2,4] possible splits - none
For [1,2,4][1, 2, 4][1,2,4] possible splits - [1,2,4][1, 2, 4][1,2,4], [1][1][1] [2,4][2, 4][2,4]
